page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 150 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the m5ad.xlarge delivers modest compute and memory performance. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 10% of performance categories, which is mirrored by Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth write speeds and database operations also rank in the bottom 10%. However, the processor demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5ad.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile with dedicated CPU allocation and local NVMe storage at a lower entry point compared to newer generation instances. The primary tradeoff is the older AMD EPYC hardware architecture, which limits raw processing throughput and memory bandwidth. This instance is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, light database operations, and development environments that benefit from local scratch space but do not require high-frequency compute power.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a 150 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate modest capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally scoring in the lower percentiles, though encryption tasks perform at an average level due to hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions. The instance represents a cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated vCPUs and local NVMe storage without demanding high-frequency compute performance. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, development environments, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for workloads requiring dedicated resources.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe SSD storage for basic computing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 150 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Hardware-accelerated encryption with AES and SHA instruction sets
Suited for general-purpose applications and development environments
